ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code for the country */,
  "continent" TEXT /* The continent to which the country belongs */,
  "region" TEXT /* The region to which the country belongs */,
  "population" INTEGER /* The approximate population of the country */,
  "capital_city" TEXT /* The capital city of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "nuts_regions" (
  "nuts_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each NUTS region */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the NUTS region belongs to */,
  "nuts_code" TEXT /* NUTS code for the region */,
  "nuts_name" TEXT /* Name of the NUTS region */,
  "nuts_level" TEXT /* The level of the NUTS region (e.g., NUTS 1, NUTS 2, NUTS 3) */,
  "parent_nuts_id" INTEGER /* The ID of the parent NUTS region (for hierarchical relationships) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("nuts_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_nuts_regions_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "case_data" (
  "case_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each case data entry */,
  "nuts_id" INTEGER /* ID of the NUTS region the case data belongs to */,
  "cases" INTEGER /* Number of cases reported */,
  "cases_lower" INTEGER /* Lower bound of cases reported */,
  "cases_upper" INTEGER /* Upper bound of cases reported */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* Date and time of the case data report */,
  "disease_type" TEXT /* The type of disease being tracked (e.g., COVID-19, influenza) */,
  "source" TEXT /* The source of the case data (e.g., government reports, news articles) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes or comments about the case data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_case_data_nuts_id FOREIGN KEY ("nuts_id") REFERENCES nuts_regions ("nuts_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, viewer) */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* The first name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* The last name of the user */,
  "affiliation" TEXT /* The organization or institution the user is affiliated with */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* The date and time of the user's last login */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the case data being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the case data */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the case data was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "action_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the access event (e.g., changes made to case data) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES case_data ("case_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the case data related to the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* The notification message sent to the user */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* The date and time the notification was sent */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES case_data ("case_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "case_data_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change event */,
  "case_id" INTEGER /* ID of the case data being modified */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the change */,
  "changed_field" TEXT /* The field that was changed (e.g., cases, cases_lower, cases_upper) */,
  "old_value" TEXT /* The previous value of the changed field */,
  "new_value" TEXT /* The new value of the changed field */,
  "datetime" TEXT /* The date and time the change was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_case_data_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_case_data_history_case_id FOREIGN KEY ("case_id") REFERENCES case_data ("case_id")
)
